message:
fuzz: add escape_parser_raw target for byte-stream fuzzing of the VT state machine (ft-1p752)

The existing escape-parser fuzz targets (termwiz_csi_parser,
osc_marker_parser) are structure-aware — they constrain the input
grammar to the CSI and OSC subsets respectively, which misses whole
classes of pathological traffic: SOS/PM/APC envelope bytes, raw DCS /
Sixel sequences, nested ESC chains, and the full ISO 2022 shift-state
space. Those paths run inside Parser::parse on production traffic; the
structured fuzzers never reach them because they can't be expressed as
a CSI or OSC grammar.

## New target: escape_parser_raw

fuzz/fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs (new file)

Feeds arbitrary bytes (bounded to 64 KiB per iteration to keep the
iteration latency cost tractable) into
frankenterm_escape_parser::parser::Parser with a counting callback.
Pins three invariants on top of the crash oracle that libfuzzer gives
for free:

  1. Parser::new produces a fresh state every iteration — no
     cross-input bleed. Each iteration constructs a new Parser so a
     previous input's mid-ESC state cannot influence the next.

  2. parse() and parse_as_vec() agree on Action counts for the same
     bytes. These are two public entry points into the same state
     machine; if the counts ever diverge, some internal fast-path has
     forked from the slow-path and the structured fuzzers would not
     catch it (because they only exercise one of the two APIs).

  3. Action count is bounded by input length × MAX_ACTIONS_PER_BYTE
     (=8). The real grammar emits at most one Action per terminal
     transition; 8 is loose enough that traffic close to the real
     bound does not trip the check, but tight enough to catch any
     regression that turns parse() into quadratic or explosive output.

## Wiring

fuzz/Cargo.toml: add [[bin]] entry with
  name = "escape_parser_raw"
  path = "fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs"
  doc = false

Does NOT opt into required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"] because
frankenterm_escape_parser is a leaf crate with no optional feature
gates — the target compiles under every feature matrix.

## Seed corpus (6 files, fuzz/corpus/escape_parser_raw/)

Minimal seed set covering the grammatical classes that the structured
fuzzers don't reach. Each is a small fixture (2..17 bytes) so libfuzzer
can mutate aggressively from a known-interesting starting point rather
than spending the first N million iterations rediscovering the alphabet:

  - apc      — APC introducer (ESC _) followed by minimal payload
  - ascii    — plain ASCII baseline
  - csi_red  — CSI "ESC [ 3 1 m" set-foreground-red
  - (plus 3 more: dcs, osc, plain_esc — not separately listed here
    because they're one-liner fixtures)

Future additions (not in this commit): Sixel framing, ISO 2022 shift
sequences, nested DCS, and PM/SOS envelope chains. Filed as follow-up
scope under ft-1p752; tight scope here to keep the initial target
small enough to land cleanly.

Tracks ft-1p752 (raw-byte escape parser fuzz coverage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -145,5 +145,10 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/ipc_auth_envelope.rs"
 doc = false
 required-features = ["core-fuzz-targets"]
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "escape_parser_raw"
+path = "fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [profile.release]
 debug = 1

@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+_apc payload\
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+[31m
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+P+q53\
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+]0;title
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+#![no_main]
+//! Crash detector for raw bytes → `frankenterm_escape_parser::Parser` (ft-1p752).
+//!
+//! The crate's existing fuzz targets are structure-aware:
+//!   * `termwiz_csi_parser.rs` — CSI grammar
+//!   * `osc_marker_parser.rs`  — OSC markers
+//!
+//! Neither exercises SOS/PM/APC, Sixel, nested DCS, or pathological ESC
+//! chains as plain byte streams. This target feeds arbitrary bytes (bounded
+//! to 64 KiB per iteration) into the full `Parser::parse` state machine
+//! with a counting callback and pins three invariants on top of the crash
+//! oracle:
+//!
+//! 1. **Parser::new produces a fresh state every iteration** (no
+//!    cross-input bleed).
+//! 2. **`parse` + `parse_as_vec` agree on Action counts** — the two
+//!    public entry points must produce the same number of Actions for
+//!    the same bytes. A divergence means the state machine has a
+//!    forked code path that the structured fuzzers would miss.
+//! 3. **Action count is bounded by input length × small constant** —
+//!    the state machine never emits more Actions than there are bytes
+//!    × the max Actions-per-byte the grammar permits (we use 8 as a
+//!    loose upper bound; tighter pinning is a follow-up). Catches any
+//!    regression that turns parse into quadratic/explosive output.
+
+use frankenterm_escape_parser::{Action, parser::Parser};
+use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
+
+/// Loose upper bound on Actions-per-byte. Real grammar emits at most
+/// one Action per state-machine terminal transition; giving headroom
+/// so this check catches true explosion, not close-to-bound traffic.
+const MAX_ACTIONS_PER_BYTE: usize = 8;
+
+fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
+    // Bound input size — beyond 64 KiB the iteration-latency cost
+    // dominates the marginal coverage gain.
+    if data.len() > 64 * 1024 {
+        return;
+    }
+
+    // Entry point 1: streaming callback form. `Parser::parse` drives the
+    // state machine, invoking the callback for each emitted Action.
+    let mut parse_callback_count: usize = 0;
+    let mut parser = Parser::new();
+    parser.parse(data, |_action: Action| {
+        parse_callback_count += 1;
+    });
+
+    // Entry point 2: batched form. Should produce the same Action count
+    // for the same bytes on a fresh parser.
+    let mut parser2 = Parser::new();
+    let vec_actions = parser2.parse_as_vec(data);
+
+    // Invariant: callback count matches batched count. If the two
+    // forms ever diverge, there's an emit-vs-collect bug that the
+    // structured fuzzers would miss because they only check one.
+    assert_eq!(
+        parse_callback_count,
+        vec_actions.len(),
+        "parse() emitted {} actions via callback but parse_as_vec() produced {} \
+         for input of {} bytes — the two entry points must agree",
+        parse_callback_count,
+        vec_actions.len(),
+        data.len()
+    );
+
+    // Invariant: output is linear-bounded. data.len() * MAX_ACTIONS_PER_BYTE
+    // is a loose ceiling; any regression that blows through it means the
+    // state machine is emitting duplicate / recursive Actions.
+    let ceiling = data.len().saturating_mul(MAX_ACTIONS_PER_BYTE).saturating_add(8);
+    assert!(
+        vec_actions.len() <= ceiling,
+        "parse_as_vec emitted {} actions on {} input bytes — exceeds linear \
+         ceiling of {} (MAX_ACTIONS_PER_BYTE = {}). Possible output explosion.",
+        vec_actions.len(),
+        data.len(),
+        ceiling,
+        MAX_ACTIONS_PER_BYTE
+    );
+});
